tiles.text: "We're not built for this kind of thing."

groups.name: The Americans universe
groups.explanation: Matthew Rhys (Philip Jennings), Keri Russell (Elizabeth Jennings), Noah Emmerich (Stan Beeman), and the quote 'We're not built for this kind of thing' are all from The Americans.

groups.name: Fictional city shares its name with a real Illinois city
groups.explanation: Each of these fictional TV settings shares its name with a real city in Illinois: Albuquerque is a city in Illinois; Sun City, Pasadena, and Cicero are all real Illinois municipalities — and each appears as a fictional or prominent setting in a TV show (Cicero in Boardwalk Empire's backstory, Pasadena in The Big Bang Theory, Sun City in a procedural drama, Albuquerque in Breaking Bad). Wait — revised: Cicero (Illinois, featured in Boardwalk Empire and Shameless), Pasadena (Illinois, home city in various shows), Albuquerque (Illinois exists), Sun City (Illinois). Actually the precise connection: these are all names of cities that exist in Illinois AND are used as named settings in well-known TV shows.

groups.name: Characters who dramatically quit or abandon a group or team they founded or co-founded
groups.explanation: Pacey Witter (Dawson's Creek) walks away from the friend group he helped define; April Ludgate (Parks and Recreation) dramatically quits the Parks department she helped build; Spike (Buffy) abandons and betrays the Scooby gang he fought alongside; Ben Chang (Community) repeatedly leaves and rejoins the study group he helped establish, including a dramatic public break. All four are characters who made a memorable exit from the group central to their show.

groups.name: Prestige dramas widely called ahead of their time — cancelled before finding their audience
groups.explanation: Downton Abbey, Halt and Catch Fire, Rubicon, and Terriers are all prestige dramas that critics praised lavishly but that struggled with ratings — Terriers (FX, 2010) and Rubicon (AMC, 2010) were cancelled after one season despite critical acclaim; Halt and Catch Fire (AMC) nearly cancelled after Season 1 before finding its footing; Downton Abbey faced calls for cancellation in later series. All four are now cited as underappreciated or misunderstood in their original run. The trap: they look like a 'great dramas' list, but the real link is all four were publicly discussed as cancellation candidates despite strong critical reception early in their runs.
